tools.name: ImageDescription
tools.description: A useful tool that returns a brief description of the input image.

tools.name: OCR
tools.description: This tool can recognize all text on the input image.

outputs.description: OCR results, include bbox in x1, y1, x2, y2 format and the recognized text.

tools.name: RegionAttributeDescription
tools.description: Describe the attribute of a region of the input image.

inputs.name: bbox
inputs.description: The bbox coordinate in the format of `(x1, y1, x2, y2)`

tools.name: GoogleSearch
tools.description: Searches the web for brief reference information to interpret domain-specific labeling or measurement conventions.

inputs.name: k
inputs.description: Number of results or snippets to return

dialogs.content: Using the provided figure, identify the methods used to detect metastases in sentinel nodes.

dialogs.thought: First orient to what the figure depicts so I can infer whether it compares histologic and molecular detection methods.

content.content: A grayscale 3D-style bar chart with categories along the x-axis labeled H&E, CK-18, CEA, hTRT, MUC-1, and Total; each bar has a number and percentage above it. Y-axis ranges up to about 30.

dialogs.thought: Extract the assay and marker labels from the figure to determine which detection methods are explicitly shown.

arguments.query: How are mass sizes typically annotated on CT images, and what format do size measurements appear in when labeled directly on a scan?

dialogs.thought: Clarify how mass sizes are typically annotated on CT images and what numeric values or units to look for when identifying a size measurement.

content.content: Mass sizes on CT are typically labeled as linear dimensions placed near measurement calipers, commonly shown in millimeters or centimeters and often written as one number or paired dimensions such as 12 mm or 2.3 x 1.8 cm.

dialogs.thought: Confirm that the x-axis labels correspond to histologic H&E evaluation and molecular marker assays used for metastasis detection.

content.content: The x-axis shows assay/marker labels: H&E, CK-18, CEA, hTRT, MUC-1, and Total, consistent with histologic H&E evaluation and molecular marker-based detection.

dialogs.content: The image shows three methods used to detect metastases in sentinel nodes: conventional histology, hematoxylin and eosin (H&E) staining, and RT-PCR and Southern blot assay.

whitelist: The image shows three methods used to detect metastases in sentinel nodes: conventional histology, hematoxylin and eosin (H&E) staining, and RT-PCR and Southern blot assay.
